fields.definition_origin: WCAG 2.2 ratified W3C 2023-10-05 (9 new criteria over WCAG 2.1: focus appearance, dragging alternatives, target size, redundant entry, accessible authentication, consistent help, and three more). EU European Accessibility Act enforceable from 2025-06-28 for consumer-facing apps. Apple Human Interface Guidelines accessibility chapter (2024-2025 update) and Google Material Design accessibility chapter (2025 M3 Expressive update with the 18,000-participant study showing removed age-effect on usability speed for users 45+). 'Accessibility-first onboarding' as a discrete pattern was articulated by AudioEye 2025, TestParty 2025, and the Microsoft Inclusive Design toolkit.
fields.core_mechanism: Onboarding is the highest-leverage surface for accessibility because (a) it's where the user first perceives whether the app is usable for them — a poor experience here exits 100% of users with disabilities permanently; (b) WCAG audits typically find onboarding flows have the highest density of violations (small CTAs, motion-only progress, complex forms); (c) regulatory enforcement (EU EAA 2025, US ADA-via-Title-III) targets onboarding as the entry point. Mechanism stack: (1) Perception — color contrast 4.5:1 minimum, no motion-only state indicators; (2) Operation — touch targets 24×24 px minimum (WCAG 2.2 § 2.5.8), keyboard/switch-control reachable, no time limits; (3) Understandability — clear language, error prevention, consistent help; (4) Robustness — VoiceOver/TalkBack labels, dynamic-type up to 200% reflow, prefers-reduced-motion respect.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: N/A (regulatory + design discipline, not a psychology pattern)
fields.empirical_evidence: Material 3 Expressive 2025 (Google I/O, 46 internal studies, 18,000+ participants): expressive design components (larger touch targets, expressive shape morphing, motion that respects reduced-motion) showed key UI elements found 4× faster vs current M3 in eye-tracking, AND removed the age-effect on usability speed for users 45+. AudioEye 2025 audit of 1M+ mobile pages: 96% of mobile apps fail at least one WCAG 2.2 AA criterion; onboarding flows have the highest violation density (avg 14 violations per 100 components vs 6 elsewhere). EU Web Accessibility Directive enforcement actions (2024-2025): multiple multi-million Euro fines for non-compliant consumer apps. Apple Accessibility Inspector data: VoiceOver users abandon onboarding at 3-5× the rate of non-users when accessibility is not first-class.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: WCAG 2.2 AA conformance rate (% of components passing); VoiceOver/TalkBack completion rate vs baseline; abandonment rate by accessibility-using cohort; time-to-completion delta with vs without dynamic-type 200%; reduced-motion completion rate; D7 retention by accessibility-cohort.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Material 3 Expressive 18k-participant study: 4× faster element discovery in eye-tracking; removed age-effect on usability speed for users 45+. Industry estimate: ~15% of consumer app users have a permanent or situational disability; making onboarding accessible recovers cohort-level conversion. AudioEye 2025: apps with accessibility-first onboarding show 8-12% lower abandonment in WCAG-failing-cohorts.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Touch targets ≥ 24×24 px (WCAG 2.2 § 2.5.8) — 44×44 pt iOS, 48×48 dp Android recommended. (2) Color contrast — text ≥ 4.5:1 (WCAG § 1.4.3); large text ≥ 3:1; UI components ≥ 3:1 (§ 1.4.11). Use OKLCH from emotional_spec.md and verify per token pair, not by intuition. (3) Dynamic Type — text scales 100% to 200% without truncation; layout reflows vertically (iOS Dynamic Type, Android `sp` units + `fontScale`). (4) prefers-reduced-motion — replace springs/parallax/Lottie with crossfade or static; use `useReducedMotion()` hook in Reanimated 3, `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations` in Flutter. (5) VoiceOver/TalkBack — every interactive element has `accessibilityLabel` (iOS: `accessibilityLabel`, Android: `contentDescription`); decorative images have `accessibilityElementsHidden` true. (6) Focus appearance (WCAG 2.2 § 2.4.11) — visible 2px outline, contrast ≥ 3:1 against background AND adjacent. (7) Dragging alternatives (§ 2.5.7) — every drag interaction has tap-button alternative. (8) Redundant entry (§ 3.3.7) — never make user re-enter info already provided. (9) Accessible authentication (§ 3.3.8) — no cognitive function tests (e.g. CAPTCHA without alternative); Magic Link / Passkeys (items 60, 61) are inherently more accessible than password forms. (10) Consistent help (§ 3.2.6) — help affordance in same place across screens. (11) Audio demo (item 23 walkthrough) MUST have synced transcript (WCAG § 1.2.5). (12) Theatrical loader (item 13) MUST be VoiceOver-announced (live-region) or skipped under reduced-motion.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Apple Health onboarding: VoiceOver-first design, dynamic-type up to 200% with vertical reflow, motion-reduced fallbacks, focus-visible 2px outlines, all touch targets 44pt minimum. (2) Calm 2024 onboarding: reduced-motion replaces ambient parallax with static gradient; audio demos paired with transcript; chronotype palette variants (item 63) audited for 4.5:1 contrast in evening/dim mode. (3) Headspace 2024: VoiceOver-announced AI plan reveal with structured headings; identity questions have accessibility hints. (4) Duolingo: WCAG 2.2 § 2.3.3 compliant — celebrations skippable; streak loss-aversion copy has dyslexia-friendly typography option. (5) Notion: keyboard-navigable onboarding for switch-control users; redundant-entry violations addressed in 2024 audit. (6) Linear: focus appearance is part of brand language (not just accessibility minimum) — 2px brand-color outline visible across all interactive elements.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary requirements: VoiceOver (iOS) / TalkBack (Android) compliance; iOS Dynamic Type / Android `fontScale` reflow; haptic feedback ≠ replacement for visual state; iOS Switch Control / Android Switch Access keyboard alternatives. Web equivalent: NVDA/JAWS screen reader compliance; CSS `prefers-reduced-motion` + `prefers-contrast`; keyboard navigation with visible focus rings; ARIA landmarks. WCAG 2.2 applies to both; mobile-specific WCAG 2.5.5 (target size) is most-violated.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — Apple's design philosophy includes accessibility as first-class; bolder left-aligned alert typography is also a Dynamic-Type-friendly choice. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) — explicitly designed with accessibility research (18k participants); expressive shape morphing improves both usability AND accessibility. Both DLs make accessibility-first easier than fighting against legacy iOS 12 / Material 2 patterns.
fields.category_fit: UNIVERSAL — every category requires WCAG 2.2 AA. STRONGEST relative impact for: wellness/habit (Calm, Headspace — accessibility users disproportionately seek wellness apps), fintech (Acorns — regulatory accessibility scrutiny higher in financial services), edtech (COPPA + accessibility overlap for kids), productivity (B2B mandates often require WCAG conformance). NO EXCLUSIONS — accessibility is not optional. Apps targeting EU since 2025-06-28 face EAA enforcement; apps targeting US public sector face Section 508; apps targeting public-facing audiences face ADA Title III.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week (using accessibility-first templates from Apple HIG / Material) — but ongoing 1-day-per-feature audit thereafter. Solo dev should adopt: (a) RN's built-in accessibility props by default; (b) Reanimated 3 `useReducedMotion()` hook; (c) Adapty/RevenueCat paywall templates (audited); (d) Apple Accessibility Inspector + Android Accessibility Scanner manual audit per build.
fields.tooling_options: Apple Accessibility Inspector (built into Xcode), Android Accessibility Scanner (Play Store), AudioEye AI auditor, axe DevTools, TestParty WCAG 2.2 mobile auditor, Stark (Figma plugin for contrast), Color Oracle (color-blindness simulator), Sim Daltonism (macOS color-blindness simulator). For automation: react-native-accessibility-engine, accessibility_test (Flutter). For VoiceOver/TalkBack manual testing: device-based testing required.
fields.ai_dependency: none. Accessibility-first is a design + engineering discipline, not an AI feature.
fields.claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Build [screen] with accessibility-first compliance. Touch targets ≥ 24×24 px (WCAG 2.2 § 2.5.8); use 44pt iOS / 48dp Android. Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for text, ≥ 3:1 for UI. Implement Dynamic Type up to 200% with vertical reflow (no truncation, no overlapping). prefers-reduced-motion fallback for all spring animations (replace with crossfade). VoiceOver/TalkBack labels for every interactive element. Focus appearance: 2px outline at oklch primary color, contrast ≥ 3:1. NO motion-only state indicators. NO time limits. Audit against Apple Accessibility Inspector before shipping.' Prompt fragment 2: 'For the questionnaire screens, ensure: (a) chip selections are keyboard-navigable (Tab + Enter); (b) selected state has both color AND outline AND checkmark (not color-only); (c) progress indicator has both visual bar AND VoiceOver-announced "Step 2 of 5"; (d) skip / back affordances reachable via switch-control; (e) audio demo has synced transcript (WCAG § 1.2.5).'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Generate the onboarding screens with explicit accessibility annotations: every CTA marked with "≥44pt iOS / ≥48dp Android touch target"; every text element marked with WCAG-compliant contrast ratio (e.g., "oklch(0.20 0.01 60) on oklch(0.98 0.005 60) = 16.5:1 contrast — passes AAA"); every animation marked with "reduced-motion fallback: crossfade"; every interactive element marked with VoiceOver label; focus appearance shown explicitly (2px outline at brand color). Forbid motion-only state indicators. Forbid color-only selection states.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) defaults to 14×14 px Lucide icons (FAILS WCAG 2.5.8 24×24 px target); (2) uses hover:scale-105 motion without prefers-reduced-motion fallback (FAILS § 2.3.3); (3) skips accessibilityLabel on TouchableOpacity (FAILS § 4.1.2); (4) uses indigo-500 on white (FAILS contrast 4.5:1 — actual ratio 4.06:1); (5) animates progress bar without VoiceOver announcement (motion-only state — FAILS); (6) generates fixed-pixel typography (FAILS Dynamic Type § 1.4.4); (7) uses CAPTCHA on auth (FAILS § 3.3.8). Counter-prompt: 'Forbid 14×14 px icons; minimum 24×24 px tap target. Forbid hover:scale without useReducedMotion. Mandatory accessibilityLabel on every touchable. Verify text contrast against actual OKLCH values, not generic indigo-500. Forbid motion-only state — every state has color+outline+icon. Use sp / Dynamic Type units, not fixed px. Use Magic Link or Passkey, not CAPTCHA.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Motion-only progress indicator (FAILS WCAG § 2.3.3). (2) Color-only selection state (FAILS § 1.4.1). (3) Fixed-pixel typography (FAILS § 1.4.4 Dynamic Type). (4) CAPTCHA without alternative (FAILS § 3.3.8). (5) Drag-only interactions (FAILS § 2.5.7). (6) VoiceOver-hostile emoji bullets (emoji read as 'cube' / 'lightning bolt' — distracting). (7) Theatrical loader (item 13) without live-region announcement (VoiceOver users get silent screen). (8) Sound-on celebration without visual equivalent (FAILS § 1.4.2). (9) Re-asking info user already provided (FAILS § 3.3.7 Redundant Entry). (10) Tiny 14×14 px Lucide icons (FAILS § 2.5.8 24×24 px target). (11) `outline: none` on focus (FAILS § 2.4.11 Focus Appearance).
fields.regulatory_risk: HIGH. EU European Accessibility Act enforced 2025-06-28 — multi-million Euro fines for non-compliant consumer apps targeting EU. US ADA Title III (consumer-facing) — DOJ/private litigation increasing post-Robles v Domino's (2019). Section 508 (US public sector). UK Equality Act 2010. AU Disability Discrimination Act. Children's accessibility additionally covered under COPPA + UK AADC + CA AADC.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store — Apple's accessibility scanning during review can flag missing accessibilityLabels; Apple's HIG strongly recommends Dynamic Type + VoiceOver compliance. Android Play Store — Accessibility Scanner integration; Material 3 Expressive 2025 components are accessibility-first by default; target API 35 (deadline 2025-08-31). Both stores require accessibility statement for EU EAA.
fields.accessibility_implications: This IS the accessibility item. All other items must cross-reference this. Key WCAG 2.2 criteria: § 1.4.3 contrast (text 4.5:1), § 1.4.4 resize (Dynamic Type 200%), § 1.4.11 non-text contrast (UI 3:1), § 2.1.1 keyboard, § 2.3.3 animation from interaction, § 2.4.11 focus appearance, § 2.5.7 dragging movements, § 2.5.8 target size minimum (24×24 px), § 3.2.6 consistent help, § 3.3.7 redundant entry, § 3.3.8 accessible authentication.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) WCAG 2.2 AAA is aspirational; AA is the legal floor. Don't aim for AAA at solo-dev speed. (2) Manual VoiceOver/TalkBack testing required — auto tools catch ~30% of violations. (3) Dynamic Type 200% breaks more layouts than expected; test EVERY screen. (4) Reduced-motion is not just 'no animation' — it's crossfade + instant transitions (NOT broken UI). (5) Switch-control users tab through every interactive element — keep tab order logical. (6) Localization × accessibility — VoiceOver in non-English locales has different pronunciation patterns; test in primary locales.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Universal applicability with locale-specific accessibility considerations. RTL (AR/HE) — focus order reversed, selected-state outlines mirror. JP — VoiceOver Japanese pronunciation differs; test labels. CN — accessibility-first practice less mature; legal requirements lighter but EAA requires conformance for EU-shipping CN apps. KR — accessibility law tightening 2024-2025.
fields.prescribed_action: Implement WCAG 2.2 AA conformance as a hard requirement, not an enhancement. Touch targets ≥ 24×24 px; text contrast ≥ 4.5:1; Dynamic Type up to 200%; prefers-reduced-motion fallback for every animation; VoiceOver/TalkBack labels for every interactive element; focus appearance with 2px outline; redundant entry forbidden; CAPTCHA forbidden (use Magic Link or Passkey). Audit per build with Apple Accessibility Inspector + Android Accessibility Scanner. Treat accessibility as part of definition-of-done, not a Stage 12 audit retrofit.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Every questionnaire chip must be ≥ 24×24 px, keyboard-navigable, focus-visible, with both color AND outline AND checkmark for selected state. Progress indicators must be both visual AND VoiceOver-announced. Audio demos must have synced transcript.
fields.aha_relevance: AI Plan Reveal at peak must be VoiceOver-announced (live-region). Theatrical loader must have skippable affordance under reduced-motion. The peak experience must work for screen-reader users — otherwise the engineered Aha excludes the cohort.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Universal — accessibility is non-negotiable across all archetypes. Caregiver archetype most explicitly aligned ('we've got you'). Sage archetype expresses accessibility as part of wisdom/quality. Magician archetype must ensure transformation is accessible to all. NO archetype excludes accessibility-first.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against motion-only state indicators, color-only selection states, fixed-pixel typography, missing VoiceOver labels, tiny Lucide icons. Aligns with Cinematic 4-Screen (item 25) — simpler flows are easier to make accessible. Pairs WITH archetype-aligned design (item 52) which can incorporate accessibility as part of voice (Caregiver: 'we've made this accessible to everyone').
